commits.subject: fix(modules): resolve 5 minor issues from third review pass
commits.body:
- Side effect in state updater: recordUpload (localStorage write)
  was inside a setUploadState callback. React StrictMode calls
  updaters twice. Moved side effect outside the updater.
- Duplicate source note on retry: handleUploadModules created a
  [Source:] note on every call including retries. Added isRetry
  flag to skip note creation on retry calls.
- Hash not recorded after successful retry: handleRetryFailed
  never called recordUpload after all items succeeded. Added
  hash recording after retry completes.
- Stale comment: referenced old constant name BATCH_SIZE instead
  of UPLOAD_BATCH_SIZE.
- Misleading JSDoc: onRetryFailed prop described as "number of
  failed items" but is a callback.

Tags: bugfix, quality, modules, upload
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: contract-upload
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KQFyjLRpK1QAMT31nJY4qz
commits.title: resolve 5 minor issues from third review pass

commits.subject: fix(modules): resolve 10 issues in contract upload feature
commits.body:
Bugs fixed:
- Stale props: ContractUploadPreview ignored new candidates when
  sub-section toggle changed. Added key prop to force remount.
- Duplicate flow infinite loop: "Upload anyway" re-opened file picker
  which re-triggered duplicate detection. Now stores ParseResult in
  duplicate state and proceeds directly to preview.
- Retry progress tracking: retrying failed modules replaced the entire
  progress array with a shorter one, losing successful items. Now merges
  retry results back at the correct indices.
- hardSplitAtWordBoundary title count: used pre-filter chunk count in
  titles like "(2/5)" when only 3 chunks existed. Now filters first.
- handleSplitOversized double computation: called splitOversizedSection
  twice. Now computes once and reuses.

Standards fixes:
- Touch targets: preview move buttons were 36px, below WCAG 2.5.5
  minimum 44px. Matched to existing ModuleCard pattern.
- Duplicated MODULE_CONTENT_LIMIT: exported from contractSplitter,
  imported in ContractUploadPreview instead of redeclaring.
- BATCH_SIZE inside component: moved to module scope as UPLOAD_BATCH_SIZE.
- PDF type annotation: replaced complex conditional type inference with
  simple let + await pattern, letting TypeScript infer the type.
- recordUpload on partial failure: hash was recorded even when some
  modules failed, causing false duplicate warnings on retry. Now only
  records on full success.

Tags: bugfix, quality, accessibility, modules, upload
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: contract-upload
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KQFyjLRpK1QAMT31nJY4qz
commits.title: resolve 10 issues in contract upload feature

commits.subject: feat(modules): add contract upload and conversion to modules
commits.body:
Upload traditional contracts (.txt, .docx, .pdf) and convert them into
Sancio's module-based structure. Client-side extraction with no backend
changes needed.

Key design decisions:
- PDF library (pdfjs-dist) lazy-loaded via dynamic import with Vite-bundled
  worker — zero cost for non-PDF users, works offline (no CDN dependency)
- Heuristic section splitting at depth 0 only by default to avoid module
  proliferation; sub-section splitting is opt-in
- Batch creation in parallel groups of 4 via Promise.allSettled to handle
  SA mobile latency on 30+ module uploads
- Source tracking via [Source: filename] note convention — syncs to both
  parties via Supabase, searchable, no schema changes needed
- Duplicate detection via text hash in localStorage
- Encoding detection: BOM → UTF-8 strict → Windows-1252 fallback
- Oversized sections (>10k chars) split via paragraph → sentence →
  word-boundary fallback chain

New files:
- src/utils/documentParser.ts — text extraction for .txt/.docx/.pdf
- src/utils/contractSplitter.ts — heuristic section splitting
- src/components/modules/ContractUploadPreview.tsx — preview/edit UI

Modified files:
- src/components/modules/TemplateSelector.tsx — tabbed UI with upload tab
- src/pages/AgreementDetailPage.tsx — batch creation handler

Tags: feature, modules, upload, parsing, pdf, docx
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: contract-upload
Semver: minor

commits.subject: fix(api): correct API contract mismatches found during review
commits.body:
Breaking fixes:
- PUT /proposals/:id: response_content → responseContent (camelCase)
- POST /pdf-verifications: agreement_id → agreementId (camelCase)

Data loss prevention:
- Module intent/summary fields: split into API fields (title, content,
  nudge, expire) + direct Supabase for unsupported fields (intent,
  summary_does, summary_when, summary_consequence)
- Note/proposal sender_tone: create via API then update tone via Supabase
- Both are temporary until tool-till-tees validation is updated

Architecture fix:
- GET endpoints for agreements, modules, notes, proposals, pdf-verifications
  require parent ID params — restored Supabase for global initial fetch
  (hooks use global store + client-side filtering)
- Notifications and signatures work without params — kept on API
- Removed dead resetSequenceSignatures (unused, sent empty body)
- Cleaned up createAgreement to not send server-determined fields

Tags: bugfix, api, migration
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: added
Epic: api-migration
Semver: patch

commits.subject: feat(api): migrate data layer from direct Supabase to tool-till-tees API
commits.body:
All 8 data hooks now route mutations through the tool-till-tees REST API
instead of direct Supabase queries. Supabase client retained for auth and
realtime subscriptions only.

New src/api/ layer:
- client.ts: base fetch wrapper with auth headers and error normalization
- Per-resource modules: projects, agreements, modules, proposals, notes,
  notifications, signatures, pdfVerifications

Migrated hooks: useProjects, useAgreements, useModules, useProposals,
useNotes, useNotifications, useSignatureLog, usePdfVerifications.

Migrated AgreementDetailPage.tsx splitAgreement from supabase.rpc to hook.

Remaining Supabase RPC calls (need API endpoints in tool-till-tees):
- acceptHandshake (no standalone endpoint)
- moveModule (API expects full order, not direction)
- fetchVersions bulk query (no bulk versions endpoint)

Tags: api, migration, architecture, refactor
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: medium
Debt: paid

commits.subject: feat(modules): multi-word intent labels + micro-summary system
commits.body:
Phase A: Enhanced intent labels — expanded defaults from 6 to 8
(added condition, declaration; replaced protection with remedy),
increased max length to 60 chars, relaxed normalization to allow
multi-word labels and accented characters for Latin phrases.

Phase B: Micro-summary system — three optional plain-language fields
(summary_does, summary_when, summary_consequence) per module.
Creator fills them via collapsible editor (save-on-blur per field),
counterparty sees them in a summary card above clause content.
Module cards show summary_does instead of content preview when available.

Tags: feature, modules, intents, summaries, ux
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral

Semver: minor

commits.body:
Intent tags let the creator categorize what a module clause is meant to do.
Six defaults: obligation, permission, restriction, definition, process,
protection. Custom intents supported via the same label system as tones.

Generalized ToneSelector to accept configurable defaults, label, and
placeholder props so it can be reused for both tones (notes/proposals)
and intents (modules).

- ModuleEditor: IntentSelector below content, saves immediately on change
- ModuleViewer: read-only intent pills
- ModuleCard: intent pills in agreement module list
- Module type: added intent: string[]
- useModules: updateModule accepts intent field
- constants/tones.ts: DEFAULT_INTENTS, MAX_INTENTS_PER_MODULE

Database:

commits.subject: docs(ops): document shared Supabase migration process and pitfalls
commits.body:
Added the stub-file approach for pushing migrations to a shared Supabase
database. Never use `migration repair --status reverted` on another
project's migrations — it deletes them from the history table and breaks
that project's tracking. The stub approach avoids touching the history
table entirely.

Tags: documentation, supabase, migrations, operations
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 3
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: deployment
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(quality): address code audit findings across codebase
commits.body:
Implements all items from the code quality audit:

- Fix timer leak in DebugPill.tsx (ref+cleanup pattern for copy feedback)
- Replace `as unknown as Record` double-casts with typed Navigator
  interface in browser.d.ts (usePWAInstall, DebugPill)
- Add `declare module 'virtual:pwa-register/react'` to eliminate
  @ts-expect-error in usePWAUpdate
- Standardize error state across all data hooks (usePdfVerifications,
  useNotifications, useSignatureLog now expose `error` like other hooks)
- Add try/catch to ProjectDetailPage.saveEdit() and
  ModuleEditor.handleSave() with user-facing error feedback
- Split AgreementDetailPage (405 lines) into AgreementHeader,
  AgreementModulesSection, and AgreementNotesSection sub-components
- Consolidate 6 modal boolean states into single discriminated
  `ActiveModal` union type, preventing impossible states

commits.subject: feat(ux): comprehensive UX, mobile, and accessibility improvements
commits.body:
Addresses 30 UX issues across touch targets, mobile usability,
accessibility, and general polish for non-technical users.

Touch targets & buttons:
- Increase all button min-heights to 40-48px (WCAG 2.5.5)
- Fix notification bell, move up/down, delete, breadcrumb touch targets
- Add min-h-[44px] to form inputs and selects

Mobile UX:
- Module editor: reduce rows 16→8, add sticky bottom save bar
- Add unsaved changes warning (beforeunload + confirm on back)
- Fix inverted responsive text sizes (text-base sm:text-sm → text-sm)
- Remove autoFocus from modal forms (prevents keyboard pop on mobile)
- Fix notification panel width (use min() for viewport-safe sizing)
- Fix header safe-area-inset-top for notched phones
- Add PDF mobile hint ("best results on desktop")

Accessibility:
- Add focus trap to Modal (Tab/Shift+Tab cycling)
- Add skip-to-content link
- Add prefers-reduced-motion support (disable animations)
- Increase breadcrumb link tap targets with padding + hover states
- Contextual empty state icons (project/agreement/module/notFound)

Performance:
- Add React.lazy code splitting for all route pages
- Suspense fallback with loading spinner

Design polish:
- Relative timestamps in notifications ("3 min ago" vs absolute)
- Larger status badges (px-2.5 py-1 vs px-2 py-0.5)
- Allow title wrapping instead of truncation (mobile-readable)
- Desktop agreement grid (sm:grid-cols-2)
- Reduced empty state padding on mobile (py-8 vs py-12)
- Toast positioned higher with safe-area buffer
- PWA install prompt delayed 30s to let users engage first

Tags: ux, mobile, accessibility, pwa, performance

Urgency: 3

commits.subject: fix(quality): remove dead code, add error handling, fix hook violations
commits.body:
Codebase review identified several issues:
- Dead mock data files (mockAgreements, mockModules, mockProjects) never
  imported after Supabase migration — deleted
- getSession() in AuthContext had no .catch(), causing silent failures on
  network errors — added catch with loading state fallthrough
- Async handlers in AgreementDetailPage called updateAgreement without
  await and without try/catch — errors silently swallowed, toast fired
  before operation completed — now properly awaited with error toasts
- handleGenerateVerification passed unused snapshotContent parameter
  (server-side RPC ignores it) — removed dead parameter from hook type
  and call site
- useCallback called inline in JSX (Rules of Hooks violation) in 3 page
  files — extracted to component body as clearToast
- ErrorBoundary logged stack traces via console.error in production —
  gated behind import.meta.env.DEV
- Modal close button missing type="button" — could cause accidental form
  submission when Modal wraps a form

Tags: cleanup, dead-code, error-handling, hooks, security

Urgency: 2

commits.subject: Merge pull request #7 from devmade-ai/claude/debug-blank-screen-zwI3S
commits.body: Improve app boot resilience with dynamic App import and error overlay
commits.title: Merge pull request #7 from devmade-ai/claude/debug-blank-screen-zwI3S

commits.subject: fix(boot): decouple DebugPill from App and add zero-dependency error overlay
commits.body:
The blank white screen with no debug pill happens because main.tsx
statically imports both App and DebugPill. If any module in App's deep
dependency tree (react-router, supabase, pages, hooks) throws during
evaluation, the entire ES module fails and DebugPill never mounts —
leaving the user with zero diagnostic information.

Three changes to make the boot process resilient:

1. Dynamic import for App — DebugPill now mounts first via static import
   (tiny chain: React + debugLog). App loads asynchronously via import().
   If App's chunk fails, DebugPill still renders and shows the error.

2. Inline error overlay in index.html — a classic (non-module) script
   that catches errors and shows them on screen before any React code
   runs. Also includes a 10-second boot timeout to catch silent failures.

3. Boot-stage logging — debugLog calls at each boot stage (main.tsx
   executing, DebugPill mounted, App loading, App rendered, Supabase
   config status) so the debug pill shows exactly where things went wrong.

Tags: boot, debug, resilience, blank-screen
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 5
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: blank-screen-fix
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QFokShfsCKZPxvHy5SuERg
commits.title: decouple DebugPill from App and add zero-dependency error overlay

commits.subject: Merge pull request #6 from devmade-ai/claude/debug-blank-screen-Z2t3P
commits.body: Add loading indicator and improve SPA routing for GitHub Pages
commits.title: Merge pull request #6 from devmade-ai/claude/debug-blank-screen-Z2t3P

commits.subject: fix(boot): add 404.html fallback and inline loading indicator
commits.body:
GitHub Pages returns its default 404 for any path that doesn't map to a
real file. After React Router navigates to /login or /projects (client-
side), a hard refresh (pulldown on mobile) asks GitHub Pages for that
path — which doesn't exist. Without a 404.html the response is empty,
producing the blank screen the user sees (no HTML, no JS, no DebugPill).

Changes:
- deploy.yml: copy index.html → 404.html after build so GitHub Pages
  serves the SPA shell for every route
- index.html: add an inline loading spinner inside #root that is visible
  immediately while JS downloads; React's createRoot().render() replaces
  #root's children, removing it automatically. Also add <noscript>
  fallback for disabled-JS browsers.
- usePWAUpdate.ts: prefix unused swUrl param to fix TS6133

Tags: deploy, spa-routing, pwa, ux
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: blank-screen-fix
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NPNG7niNfjuNe2rdGBoiWM
commits.title: add 404.html fallback and inline loading indicator

commits.subject: feat(pwa): adopt glow-props PWA patterns — install detection, update control, race fix
commits.body:
Implements three patterns from the glow-props shared reference:

1. beforeinstallprompt race condition fix: Inline script in index.html
   captures the event before React mounts, so repeat visitors with a
   cached service worker don't lose the install prompt.

2. Browser-specific install flow: New usePWAInstall hook detects browser
   type (Chrome/Edge/Brave/Safari/Firefox) and routes to the right UX.
   Chromium gets native install; Safari/Firefox get an
   InstallInstructionsModal with per-browser step-by-step guides.
   Install analytics tracked in localStorage (last 50 events).

3. User-controlled SW updates: Switched from registerType 'autoUpdate'
   to 'prompt' via new usePWAUpdate hook. Users see a blue banner when
   an update is available and choose when to apply it — no more
   mid-session reloads. Checks for new SW versions every 60 minutes.
   Offline-ready notification auto-dismisses after 3 seconds.

Also adds glow-props as a shared reference in CLAUDE.md with fetch URL
and list of adopted patterns.

Tags: pwa, install, update, ux, glow-props
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-hardening
Semver: minor

commits.title: adopt glow-props PWA patterns — install detection, update control, race fix

commits.subject: fix(boot): prevent blank screen when Supabase env vars are missing
commits.body:
The Supabase client module threw at import time if VITE_SUPABASE_URL or
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY were not set. Because all imports in main.tsx run
before createRoot(), this killed the entire app — including the DebugPill
that was meant to survive crashes — resulting in a blank white screen with
no diagnostics.

Now supabase.ts exports an isSupabaseConfigured flag and uses placeholder
values instead of throwing. AuthContext skips Supabase calls when unconfigured,
and LoginPage shows a clear banner explaining what to do.

Tags: bugfix, boot, supabase, auth, ux

commits.subject: refactor(debug): rewrite DebugPill to match glow-props spec
commits.body:
Rewrites the debug pill to align with the spec from glow-props/CLAUDE.md:

- Separate React root (debug-root div) so the pill survives App crashes
- Centralized debugLog.ts event store: circular buffer of 200 entries with
  pub/sub, source tags (boot/db/graph/graphData/pwa/render/global/seed),
  and severity levels (info/success/warn/error)
- Module-level console.error/warn interception and window.onerror hooks
  installed at import time, before React mounts
- Two-tab expanded UI: Log (scrollable, color-coded by source & severity,
  auto-scrolls to newest, timestamps as HH:MM:SS.mmm) and Environment
  (URL, user agent, screen/viewport, online, protocol, standalone, SW
  support, IndexedDB support)
- Collapsed pill shows "dbg" label with entry count plus error/warning badges
- Copy generates full debug report (environment + all entries) with textarea
  fallback; Clear wipes all entries

commits.body:
Addresses iOS Safari issues, touch targets, accessibility, and general
UX clarity for non-technical users on mobile devices.

Mobile fixes:
- Prevent iOS auto-zoom on inputs (text-base on mobile, text-sm on desktop)
- Add viewport-fit=cover for iPhone notch/home indicator support
- Replace min-h-screen with min-h-dvh for iOS Safari dynamic toolbar
- Toast safe-area inset for iPhone home indicator
- Modal scrollable content with max-height for keyboard overlap

- Replace "Sign out" text with icon on mobile to reduce header crowding
- Reduce header gap to gap-1 on mobile
- Stack signature log IP/user-agent vertically on mobile
- Fix notification dropdown rubber-banding with overscroll-contain

UX clarity:
- Ready-to-sign toggle now says "Undo ready status" when active
- Back buttons styled as recognizable button with hover state
- Edit mode shows "Editing" badge for visual indicator
- Batch sign "Cancel" changed to "Done" for clearer intent
- "Counterparty" renamed to "Other party" in header role switcher
- Tooltip on role switcher explains counterparty meaning
- Truncated card titles now show full text on hover
- Version history shows last-updated timestamp on current version
- Expired modules: move/delete buttons disabled with explanatory tooltip

Accessibility:
- aria-describedby links form errors to their inputs
- aria-invalid on inputs with errors
- role="alert" on error messages

- Module content viewer uses break-words for long content

commits.body:
Complete Phase 7 frontend polish:
- Expired modules: greyed-out cards with "Expired" badge, warning banners,
  signing toggle hidden, batch sign excludes expired
- Input validation: character counters and limits on module content (10K),
  notes (2K), proposals (10K)
- PDF verification: loading state with disabled button during generation
- Notification triggers: wired to all key events (signing, proposals, notes,
  sequence approval, splits, status changes, handshake, agreement requests)
- Mobile: responsive notification dropdown, flex-wrap on PDF preview buttons

commits.subject: feat(phase3): add signing, notes, version history, and sequence sign-off
commits.body:
Phase 3 frontend implementation adds the complete signing and negotiation
tracking layer. Modules can now be independently signed by both parties
with auto-lock when both mark ready, batch signing for efficiency, and
amendment flow that preserves version history. Notes with source tagging
enable tracking of discussions across channels. Sequence sign-off ensures
both parties approve the final module ordering before an agreement is
fully locked.

Tags: signing, notes, version-history, ui, hooks, components

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: phase3-signing-notes-versions
Semver: minor

commits.subject: feat(foundation): implement Phase 1 frontend with React + TypeScript + Tailwind
commits.body:
Complete Phase 1 (Foundation) frontend for Sancio agreement builder.
Frontend-only with mock data and localStorage persistence, designed
for clean swap to Supabase backend when ready.

What's included:
- Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4 + PWA scaffold
- Shared type definitions (Project, Agreement, Module, User)
- Reusable component library (Button, Input, TextArea, Modal, EmptyState, etc.)
- Mock auth with AuthContext + localStorage
- CRUD hooks (useProjects, useAgreements, useModules) with localStorage
- 6 starter module templates
- All pages: Login, ProjectList, ProjectDetail, AgreementDetail, ModuleEditor, 404
- React Router v7 with protected routes and breadcrumbs
- Module reorder (up/down), inline editing, delete confirmations
- Product definition saved as docs/PRODUCT_DEFINITION.md
- Backend spec started as docs/BACKEND_SPEC.md

Tags: foundation, frontend, react, typescript, tailwind, pwa, scaffold
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: phase-1-foundation
Semver: minor
